title: Ukraine Strikes Ilsky Refinery (Krasnodar) and Leningrad Oblast Energy Facility for Second Night
detail: Ukrainian long-range drones struck Russia's Ilsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai on the night of March 26–27, setting it ablaze — the facility processes crude oil for the domestic Russian market and military fuel supply chains. Drones also struck a major energy facility in Leningrad Oblast for the second consecutive night following the March 25 Ust-Luga attack. Additional drone strikes hit targets in Kazan and Nizhnekamsk (Tatarstan), causing explosions and disrupting electricity; airports in Kazan, Nizhnekamsk, Krasnodar, Sochi, and Gelendzhik suspended operations. The Metafrax Enterprise chemical factory in Gubakha, Perm Oblast — linked to industrial explosives manufacturing — was also struck, with witnesses reporting multiple explosions and fire. Ukraine's sustained drone campaign has now extended to 9 different Russian regions in March 2026 alone.

title: Russian Airstrikes Kill 11 in Kharkiv; 509 Drones and Missiles Launched Against Ukraine
detail: Russia launched approximately 509 drones and missiles against Ukraine on March 27, 2026, killing at least 11 people in Kharkiv and causing at least 3 civilian deaths and 39 injuries across multiple regions. Ukraine's air defenses intercepted 130 of 153 drones tracked overnight. Attacks struck Donbas, Sumy, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, Odesa, and Kherson regions. In a separate incident, a naval drone attacked a Sierra Leone-flagged crude oil tanker departing Russia's Novorossiysk port just outside Turkish waters — the vessel's deck, bridge, and engine room were damaged and it began taking on water, though all 27 crew survived. Ukraine claimed responsibility for the naval drone attack as part of its campaign to disrupt Russian Black Sea oil exports.

title: Russia and Ukraine Exchange 300 Prisoners of War
detail: Russia and Ukraine conducted a prisoner of war exchange on March 27, 2026, with 300 soldiers released on each side. The exchange was facilitated through UAE-mediated backchannel communications and represents one of the larger single-day swaps of 2026. Separately, the US allocated $25 million to identify, return, and rehabilitate Ukrainian children forcibly abducted by Russia — part of a broader diplomatic effort to address ICC arrest warrant conditions against Putin and Commissioner Lvova-Belova. Diplomatic sources reported that Trump and Zelenskyy had reached agreement on approximately 90–95% of a peace proposal framework, though Russia's response was dismissive — Putin claimed Ukrainian forces had targeted his residence, and Kremlin insiders told ISW that Russia's presidential administration is planning September 2026 Duma elections on the assumption the war will still be ongoing.
